notes: Structural + media gates, graded by skill-creator's grader. The [media-judge] expectations are graded by the in-session model READING the extracted frames (exports/frames/*.png) or the MP4 — never the filename/size. hf-render renders a VALIDATED composition; it does not author or fix it. Assumes a composition/ that passed hf-validate. Derived from 1-requirements.md JTBD-1 (rendered-video row: correct dims/fps/duration; legible, on-brand, varied easing + transitions), JTBD-2 (data-viz values match), JTBD-4 (re-render / resize, facts preserved).

evals.name: render-16x9-verified-and-legible
evals.prompt: Render the api-rate-limit-teaser at 16:9 in draft quality and verify it.
expectations: ffprobe confirms codec h264, dimensions 1920x1080, a frame rate (e.g. 30/1), and a non-zero duration
extracts at least one key frame per scene into exports/frames/
[media-judge] the rendered frames are legible and faithful: the headline and key facts are present and readable, high contrast, text inside the safe zone (no clipping/overlap)
[media-judge] the composition is on-brand (the concept palette and fonts are applied, not generic defaults) and composed (clear hierarchy, edge-anchored, not a centered single element)
[media-judge] across frames around the scene transition, the motion shows varied easing and a real transition (a wipe/flash/iris), not just flat cross-fades
no HeyGen cloud/lambda/auth was used; the render ran locally

evals.name: data-viz-numbers-match-input
evals.prompt: Render the q4-revenue data-viz at 16:9 and verify the figures.
expectations: writes a verified exports/q4-revenue-16x9.mp4 (h264, 1920x1080, non-zero duration)
[media-judge] the numbers shown in the rendered frames exactly match the input quarterly figures (no fabricated or rounded-away values); digits do not jitter (tabular-nums)
[media-judge] the data-viz animates (a counter and/or a bar/ring fills) and the final figures are correct and legible

evals.name: resize-different-orientation-routes-to-build
evals.prompt: Now give me the api-rate-limit-teaser as a 9:16.
expectations: recognizes that the existing composition is 16:9 (landscape) and a 9:16 (portrait) export is a RE-AUTHORED composition, not a --resolution flag
render.sh fails the 9:16 AR cleanly with a message that --resolution cannot rotate and routes the fix to hf-build (re-author the root to 1080x1920)
does NOT produce a stretched/letterboxed or wrongly-dimensioned 9:16 file
after a portrait composition is authored, a re-render writes a verified exports/<name>-9x16.mp4 at 1080x1920 with the on-screen facts unchanged

evals.name: render-4k-same-orientation
evals.prompt: Render the api-rate-limit-teaser at 16:9 and a 4k 16:9 in one pass.
expectations: writes a verified exports/<name>-16x9.mp4 (1920x1080) AND exports/<name>-16x9-4k.mp4 (3840x2160) using --resolution landscape-4k for the 4k pass
both are h264 with non-zero duration; the 4k is an integer upscale of the same landscape composition
[media-judge] the 4k frame is sharp and legible at the larger size; the brand palette/fonts are applied
